locked_decision: Keep exact-cover PH partitioning; add PH anchor lifetime as an input channel and sample-level loss weight instead of using lifetime-weighted overlap merge.
description: CountSpatial U-Net lifetime pilot. Channel 0 remains encoded brightness, PH channels remain unchanged, and an additional anchor_lifetime_map channel gives the network absolute PH persistence confidence. The loss optionally upweights higher-lifetime patches for count, spatial, density, and background terms while preserving exact-cover full-scene merging.

patching.loss_weight: valid_crop * (0.25 + 0.75 * ph_soft_attention); sample terms optionally scaled by normalized log1p(anchor_lifetime)

partitioning.policy: PH anchors claim valid sea pixels first; fallback grid assigns every remaining valid sea pixel exactly once; halo pixels provide context only.

hierarchical_ph.policy: Large H0 parent anchors are not kept as oversized training patches. Local H0 PH is rerun inside each large parent with relaxed child detection; child anchors claim local peaks first and the existing fallback grid covers any remainder.

loss.name: count_spatial_density_loss
loss.rationale: Separates the task into total ship-count regression and spatial allocation. The model predicts one scalar count per patch and one spatial logit map. The loss uses log-count MSE for total count and KL divergence against the sum-normalized target density for localization. The density term is weak and used only to keep the merged count*spatial output numerically grounded. Count head uses exp(raw) so optimization happens naturally in log-count space instead of slowly scaling softplus outputs from near zero. This lifetime variant treats H0 lifetime as a confidence prior: it is supplied as an explicit input channel and used as a normalized log1p lifetime sample weight. Full-scene merge remains owner-mask exact-cover, so lifetime does not distort count mass during assembly.
